rationale: R1 layered model is sound and composes cleanly with the existing PanelGateDecision leg ordering. The new unresolved-RC block sits after completeness (8) and REJECT/hard_block (9) and before the threshold floor (10); every earlier short-circuit (abandoned/round-mismatch/missing-ref/transient/stale/dirty/incomplete/REJECT/hard) is byte-preserved. 'Latest-round verdict per slot' is well-defined: Tally already reduces res.Verdicts to the filename-derived latest round only (tally.go:273-276), each carrying .Slot/.Verdict. Block fires for N-1 APPROVE + 1 RC (today Approves>=threshold Allows at gate.go:257). R2 anti-gaming is airtight BY CONSTRUCTION: refutation has effect ONLY at the new RC leg, so it can never clear REJECT/hard_block (leg 9 precedes and reads independent HardBlocks/Rejects), stale (6), incomplete (8), round-mismatch (4) — all precede it — nor the sub-threshold floor (10), which still gates on genuine Approves. Refuted-RC-never-counts-as-APPROVE holds because refutation is a separate panel.json array never fed into the tally switch (tally.go:296-301), so Approves is untouched. Round-binding {slot,round}==LatestRound blocks a newer-round re-RC. Audit mirrors panel_abandoned exactly. Grounding checked line-by-line: accurate. No gaming path found.
findings: R1 airtight: unrefuted RC and unrecognized verdict strings now Block (neither is APPROVE nor refutable) — this also closes today's pre-existing hole where an unrecognized string rides through at threshold as 'neither'. Improvement, consistent with the goal.

R2 anti-gaming is enforced by the existing leg ORDER, not ad-hoc checks: because legs 2-9 short-circuit before the new RC leg and leg 10's floor reads only genuine Approves, refutation structurally cannot clear a REJECT/hard_block/incomplete/stale/round-mismatch/sub-threshold. The refuted!=APPROVE guard is the second airtight lock (separate array, tally count untouched). Verified no bypass.

NON-BLOCKING (implementers note): unrecognized-verdict-now-blocks is a behavior tightening. AC5 says 'no existing panel-gate test semantically modified' — confirm no current test pins the old unrecognized-string-rides-through-at-threshold behavior; if one exists, it is a legitimate update, not a regression. Recommend an explicit AC case for it.

NON-BLOCKING (parity footgun): a refutation with empty reason/evidence can still clear an RC (the gate matches slot+round; R2 makes empty fields 'a consumer concern'). This mirrors the accepted empty-abandon_reason footgun and ADR-0037 §8 (anti-footgun, not anti-adversary), so it is internally consistent — but the ADR amendment / SKILL text should be crisp that the reason/evidence carry the legitimacy, since the gate does not enforce non-emptiness.

NON-BLOCKING (edge): two verdict files for the same slot at the latest round would yield two Verdicts entries; a single {slot,round} refutation's clearing semantics against duplicates is a pre-existing ambiguity. Worth one decomposition/test note, not a design flaw.

Grounding verified against 472f9ed6: gate.go legs 9 (245-253) & 10 (255-262); tally.go RC-as-neither switch (296-301) + doc (107-111) + latest-round-only tally (273-276) + Verdict.Slot (43-59); panel.go ApproveThreshold (113-127) + abandon-lenience (58-68); panel_advisory.go panelGate (140-215) + writePanelAuditMetadata (304-342). All citations accurate. VoteDecision lockstep is feasible: refutation+RC data lives on *Result (res.Panel.Refutations + res.Verdicts), git-free, so a shared helper can serve both PanelGateDecision and VoteDecision.
